summary: 2026-06-27 18:18:21 PDT — Reviewed `git diff main...HEAD`, `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, and `reviews/pluggable-reviewer.md`. The reviewer commands themselves keep the codex envelope, but the branch still has residual Codex role language and the new gate has several false-green gaps around AC1/AC2/AC4/AC7. `tests/reviewer_test.sh` passes; the full configured gate could not run in this read-only sandbox because `tests/guard_test.sh` creates temp repos.

findings.title: AC6 still leaves Codex as the reviewer role
findings.claim: The skill metadata still says to "have Codex independently review the feature branch". That is role language, not a concrete CLI/key/filename reference, so it violates AC6's neutral-role requirement even though the command body correctly retains `codex exec`.
findings.suggestion: Change the role phrase to the independent/configured reviewer while keeping `codex exec`, `codexModel`, and `.codex.json` references where they mean the concrete tool/key/file. Broaden the AC6 test beyond the exact `You are Codex` phrase.

findings.title: AC4 test can pass with broken command envelopes
findings.claim: AC4 requires all three codex invocations to carry `-s read-only`, absolute `--output-schema`, repo-relative `-o`, `${codexModel:+...}`, and `</dev/null`. The test uses file-wide greps, so it would still pass if the correctness or frame command lost `-s read-only`, `${codexModel:+...}`, or `</dev/null` while another block retained the substring.
findings.suggestion: Assert the envelope per command block: approach, correctness, and frame design each need their own checks for read-only, schema path, output path, model passthrough, and stdin guard.

findings.title: AC1/AC2 fixtures are not actually exercised
findings.claim: The test only reads the live `.claude/workflow.json` and greps prose for the resolver rules. It never checks the required missing-reviewer fixture, `agy` config fixture, invalid config error, override precedence, both token orders, or invalid override token handling, so the gate can pass while AC1/AC2 examples are incomplete or contradictory.
findings.suggestion: Add explicit fixture assertions for missing/empty config => codex, config agy => agy, invalid config => stop, `/review agy` over config codex, `/review approach agy` and `/review agy approach`, and invalid bare args.

findings.title: AC7 scope whitelist is not tested
findings.claim: After AC6 the test only checks frame bootstrap and exits; it never runs the AC7 `git diff --name-only main...HEAD` whitelist required by the spec. The actual branch diff includes review/story artifacts that are not in the story's enumerated file list, so the current gate would miss a scope-containment failure.
findings.suggestion: Add an AC7 block comparing `git diff --name-only main...HEAD` to the approved whitelist, and either enumerate the expected story/review artifacts or explicitly exempt workflow artifacts in the AC.
